content.content:
1	import { afterAll, beforeAll, beforeEach, describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
2	
3	import * as extensionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/extensions"
4	import { extensions, extensionTags, organizations, tags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
5	import { parseFilters } from "~~/shared/validators/filters"
6	
7	import { setupDb, type TestDb } from "../helpers/db"
8	
9	describe("extensions repository", () => {
10	  let db: TestDb
11	  let cleanup: () => Promise<void>
12	
13	  beforeAll(async () => {
14	    const handle = await setupDb()
15	    db = handle.db
16	    cleanup = handle.cleanup
17	
18	    await db.insert(organizations).values({
19	      id: "org-1",
20	      slug: "org-1",
21	      name: "Org One",
22	    })
23	    await db.insert(tags).values([
24	      { id: "automation", labelEn: "Automation", labelZh: "自动化" },
25	      { id: "k8s", labelEn: "Kubernetes", labelZh: "K8s" },
26	    ])
27	  })
28	
29	  afterAll(async () => {
30	    await cleanup()
31	  })
32	
33	  beforeEach(async () => {
34	    // Each test gets a clean extensions table so list assertions are
35	    // independent. We don't need to clear orgs/tags — those are stable seed.
36	    await db.delete(extensionTags)
37	    await db.delete(extensions)
38	  })
39	
40	  async function seedExtension(overrides: Partial<typeof extensions.$inferInsert> = {}) {
41	    const row: typeof extensions.$inferInsert = {
42	      id: "ext-1",
43	      slug: "kubernetes-helper",
44	      category: "skills",
45	      scope: "personal",
46	      ownerOrgId: "org-1",
47	      name: "Kubernetes Helper",
48	      description: "A helper for kubernetes",
49	      visibility: "published",
50	      downloadsCount: 10,
51	      ...overrides,
52	    }
53	    await db.insert(extensions).values(row)
54	    return row.id!
55	  }
56	
57	  describe("findBySlug", () => {
58	    it("returns a published extension by slug with tag ids aggregated", async () => {
59	      await seedExtension()
60	      await db.insert(extensionTags).values([
61	        { extensionId: "ext-1", tagId: "automation" },
62	        { extensionId: "ext-1", tagId: "k8s" },
63	      ])
64	
65	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, "kubernetes-helper")
66	      expect(ext).not.toBeNull()
67	      expect(ext!.slug).toBe("kubernetes-helper")
68	      expect([...(ext!.tagIds ?? [])].sort()).toEqual(["automation", "k8s"])
69	    })
70	
71	    it("returns null for a draft extension when onlyPublished is implicit (true)", async () => {
72	      await seedExtension({ visibility: "draft" })
73	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, "kubernetes-helper")
74	      expect(ext).toBeNull()
75	    })
76	
77	    it("returns a draft extension when onlyPublished is false", async () => {
78	      await seedExtension({ visibility: "draft" })
79	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(
80	        db,
81	        "kubernetes-helper",
82	        { onlyPublished: false },
83	      )
84	      expect(ext).not.toBeNull()
85	      expect(ext!.visibility).toBe("draft")
86	    })
87	
88	    it("returns null when no extension matches", async () => {
89	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, "nonexistent")
90	      expect(ext).toBeNull()
91	    })
92	  })
93	
94	  describe("findById", () => {
95	    it("returns an extension by id regardless of visibility", async () => {
96	      await seedExtension({ visibility: "draft" })
97	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, "ext-1")
98	      expect(ext?.id).toBe("ext-1")
99	    })
100	
101	    it("returns null for an unknown id", async () => {
102	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, "nope")
103	      expect(ext).toBeNull()
104	    })
105	  })
106	
107	  describe("findManyForList + countFiltered", () => {
108	    it("returns only published extensions and counts them", async () => {
109	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-1", slug: "a", visibility: "published" })
110	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-2", slug: "b", visibility: "published" })
111	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-3", slug: "c", visibility: "draft" })
112	
113	      const filters = parseFilters({ sort: "recent", dept: "__all" })
114	      const rows = await extensionsRepo.findManyForList(db, filters)
115	      const total = await extensionsRepo.countFiltered(db, filters)
116	      expect(rows.map((r) => r.slug).sort()).toEqual(["a", "b"])
117	      expect(total).toBe(2)
118	    })
119	  })
120	
121	  describe("findFeatured", () => {
122	    it("returns the published extension with the most downloads", async () => {
123	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-low", slug: "low", downloadsCount: 5 })
124	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-high", slug: "high", downloadsCount: 50 })
125	      await seedExtension({
126	        id: "ext-draft",
127	        slug: "draft",
128	        downloadsCount: 999,
129	        visibility: "draft",
130	      })
131	
132	      const featured = await extensionsRepo.findFeatured(db)
133	      expect(featured?.slug).toBe("high")
134	    })
135	
136	    it("returns null when nothing is published", async () => {
137	      await seedExtension({ visibility: "draft" })
138	      const featured = await extensionsRepo.findFeatured(db)
139	      expect(featured).toBeNull()
140	    })
141	  })
142	
143	  describe("findRelated", () => {
144	    it("returns up to 4 published extensions of the same category, excluding self", async () => {
145	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-1", slug: "self", category: "skills" })
146	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-2", slug: "p1", category: "skills", downloadsCount: 30 })
147	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-3", slug: "p2", category: "skills", downloadsCount: 20 })
148	      await seedExtension({ id: "ext-4", slug: "mcp1", category: "mcp" })
149	      await seedExtension({
150	        id: "ext-5",
151	        slug: "draft1",
152	        category: "skills",
153	        visibility: "draft",
154	      })
155	
156	      const related = await extensionsRepo.findRelated(db, "ext-1", "skills")
157	      const slugs = related.map((r) => r.slug)
158	      expect(slugs).toEqual(["p1", "p2"])
159	    })
160	  })
161	
162	  describe("updateVisibility", () => {
163	    it("flips a draft to published with publishedAt set", async () => {
164	      await seedExtension({ visibility: "draft" })
165	      const now = new Date("2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")
166	      await extensionsRepo.updateVisibility(db, "ext-1", {
167	        visibility: "published",
168	        publishedAt: now,
169	      })
170	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, "ext-1")
171	      expect(ext?.visibility).toBe("published")
172	      expect(ext?.publishedAt?.toISOString()).toBe(now.toISOString())
173	    })
174	  })
175	
176	  describe("incrementDownloads", () => {
177	    it("adds 1 to downloads_count atomically — concurrent calls don't lose updates", async () => {
178	      await seedExtension({ downloadsCount: 0 })
179	      // SQL-level `+ 1` is what makes this safe under contention; the
180	      // assertion would fail if the function were implemented as
181	      // "read, increment in JS, write" rather than `set ... = ... + 1`.
182	      await Promise.all(
183	        Array.from({ length: 20 }, () =>
184	          extensionsRepo.incrementDownloads(db, "ext-1"),
185	        ),
186	      )
187	      const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, "ext-1")
188	      expect(ext?.downloadsCount).toBe(20)
189	    })
190	  })
191	})

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"

import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import {
  buildExtensionOrder,
  buildExtensionWhere,
} from "~~/shared/search/query"
import {
  PAGE_SIZE,
  pageOffset,
  type Filters,
} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

import type { Transactable } from "./types"

// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
// PGlite-backed integration test DB.

// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
// rename.
const listSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const detailSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
  permissions: extensions.permissions,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
  visibility: extensions.visibility,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const relatedSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
}

export async function findManyForList(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
) {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)

  return db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(...order)
    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
}

export async function countFiltered(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
): Promise<number> {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const [row] = await db
    .select({ count: count() })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(where)
  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
}

export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(1)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

input.new_string:
export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p24-elegance-repo): findFeatured prefers curated rows

- listSelect picks up tagline + taglineZh so the home hero can render a
  short editorial line without an extra detailSelect round-trip.
- findFeatured now does two passes: first prefers featured=true (newest
  publishedAt wins among ties), then falls back to top-downloaded so the
  home is never empty when nothing is curated.
- Integration tests cover curated-over-top, tie-break by publishedAt,
  draft-ignored-even-when-flagged, and the fallback path.

ExtensionListItem gains two nullable string fields — additive, no
existing consumers break. /api/v1 list shape is unchanged because that
endpoint maps fields explicitly rather than spreading.
EOF
)"

content.subject: Create feature branch
content.description: Create and switch to feat/layout-elegance-pass off main

content.subject: B.1 schema + migration for featured column
content.description: Add featured boolean default false on extensions table; generate Drizzle migration; add composite index (featured, publishedAt DESC). Commit: feat(p?-db): add featured column to extensions

content.subject: B.2 repo findFeatured + listSelect tagline
content.description: Extend listSelect with tagline/taglineZh; rewrite findFeatured to prefer featured=true with fallback to top-downloaded; integration test. Commit: feat(p?-repo): findFeatured prefers featured rows

content.subject: B.3 ExtFeatureHero + endpoint + home wiring
content.description: Create /api/internal/featured.get.ts; build ExtFeatureHero component reusing ExtHero + InstallCommand; wire into app/pages/index.vue with fallback for empty state; component test. Commit: feat(p?-ui): ExtFeatureHero on home

content.subject: A top bar slim-down + sidebar primary nav
content.description: Drop inline nav from TopBar; ghost search; 64px header in both layouts; unforce mcp-panorama collapse; add Explore section to Sidebar with 4 nav links; update Sidebar test. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): move primary nav to sidebar, ghost search, 64px header
content.activeForm: Slimming top bar and moving nav to sidebar

content.subject: C.1 FuncCategoryPicker component
content.description: New filter component mirroring DeptPicker's expandedPath + flatten() pattern; reads/writes funcCat/subCat/l2 via useFilters; slot into FilterBar; component test. Commit: feat(p?-filters): FuncCategoryPicker

content.subject: C.2 drop categories section from sidebar
content.description: Delete Sidebar.vue lines 122-208 plus related state/imports; rewrite Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts to drop Categories assertions. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): drop categories section from sidebar

content.subject: D.1 accent restraint pass
content.description: Swap sidebar active states to left-rule + neutral ink; neutralize secondary filter pickers; keep ScopePills accent; remove fallback hero gradient; smoke-test all three themes. Commit: refactor(p?-ui): restrain accent usage on secondary surfaces

content.subject: D.2 docs: accent convention
content.description: Add item 11 to CLAUDE.md Locked Product Decisions; record convention in docs/plan.md. Commit: docs: lock accent-usage convention

content.subject: Open PR
content.description: Push branch and open PR with full summary across all four phases

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p24-elegance-ui): ExtFeatureHero on home

Replace the generic gradient hero with a real editorial spotlight:
- Two-column layout (text · install line), accent only as a left-rule
  and on the "View extension" arrow — single accent moment per viewport.
- Eyebrow uses existing home.featuredLabel ("Featured this week"); name,
  tagline, description all locale-aware via nameZh/taglineZh/descriptionZh.
- Reuses InstallCommand for the terminal-style "agentcenter install …"
  line so the affordance matches the detail page.

When no extension is featured and nothing is published, the slim
fallback hero still ships (no accent gradient — that's now reserved for
the featured moment). New /api/internal/featured endpoint with 60s
s-maxage to keep the home cheap.
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit B.3: ExtFeatureHero + endpoint + home wiring

content.subject: Create feature branch
content.description: Create and switch to feat/layout-elegance-pass off main

content.subject: B.1 schema + migration for featured column
content.description: Add featured boolean default false on extensions table; generate Drizzle migration; add composite index (featured, publishedAt DESC). Commit: feat(p?-db): add featured column to extensions

content.subject: B.2 repo findFeatured + listSelect tagline
content.description: Extend listSelect with tagline/taglineZh; rewrite findFeatured to prefer featured=true with fallback to top-downloaded; integration test. Commit: feat(p?-repo): findFeatured prefers featured rows

content.subject: B.3 ExtFeatureHero + endpoint + home wiring
content.description: Create /api/internal/featured.get.ts; build ExtFeatureHero component reusing ExtHero + InstallCommand; wire into app/pages/index.vue with fallback for empty state; component test. Commit: feat(p?-ui): ExtFeatureHero on home

content.subject: A top bar slim-down + sidebar primary nav
content.description: Drop inline nav from TopBar; ghost search; 64px header in both layouts; unforce mcp-panorama collapse; add Explore section to Sidebar with 4 nav links; update Sidebar test. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): move primary nav to sidebar, ghost search, 64px header
content.activeForm: Slimming top bar and moving nav to sidebar

content.subject: C.1 FuncCategoryPicker component
content.description: New filter component mirroring DeptPicker's expandedPath + flatten() pattern; reads/writes funcCat/subCat/l2 via useFilters; slot into FilterBar; component test. Commit: feat(p?-filters): FuncCategoryPicker

content.subject: C.2 drop categories section from sidebar
content.description: Delete Sidebar.vue lines 122-208 plus related state/imports; rewrite Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts to drop Categories assertions. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): drop categories section from sidebar

content.subject: D.1 accent restraint pass
content.description: Swap sidebar active states to left-rule + neutral ink; neutralize secondary filter pickers; keep ScopePills accent; remove fallback hero gradient; smoke-test all three themes. Commit: refactor(p?-ui): restrain accent usage on secondary surfaces

content.subject: D.2 docs: accent convention
content.description: Add item 11 to CLAUDE.md Locked Product Decisions; record convention in docs/plan.md. Commit: docs: lock accent-usage convention

content.subject: Open PR
content.description: Push branch and open PR with full summary across all four phases

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(p24-elegance-layout): primary nav in sidebar, ghost search, 64px header

TopBar drops its four inline nav links (Explore, MCP Panorama, Publish,
Docs) — they now live in a new "Explore" section at the top of the
Sidebar, with lucide icons (Compass, Map, Upload, BookOpen). The search
input becomes a ghost field: borderless on a tinted background until
focus, where it surfaces a border + accent ring.

Header grows 52px → 64px (h-16) in both default and mcp-panorama
layouts. mcp-panorama gains the global aside slot, defaulting to
collapsed so the page's own LayerSidebar keeps the gutter — the menu
toggle opens it on demand, so primary nav stays reachable from the
panorama page.

i18n: new sidebar.explore key (en/zh); all four nav labels reuse the
existing nav.* keys. Docs renders as a disabled placeholder, mirroring
its previous "coming soon" treatment in TopBar.
EOF
)"

33	    expect(filters.value.tags).toEqual(["search", "api"])
34	  })

37	    const router = useRouter()

git add -A && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p24-elegance-filters): FuncCategoryPicker for funcCat / subCat / l2

Mirrors DeptPicker's expandedPath + flatten pattern over FUNC_TAXONOMY's
three-level tree. Reads/writes funcCat / subCat / l2 through useFilters,
preserving the URL contract the sidebar uses today — saved or shared
links keep working unchanged.

Slotted into FilterBar next to DeptPicker. New i18n keys
filters.funcCat.{label,all} in both locales. Color swatches reuse
FUNC_CAT_COLORS so the picker carries the same visual language as the
sidebar tree it's replacing in Phase C.2.

Tests mock useFilters via mockNuxtImport so update() call shapes are
asserted directly — sidesteps an isolated-app router quirk in
mountSuspended where router.replace doesn't propagate to the global
useRoute() the assertions read.
EOF
)"

content.subject: Create feature branch

content.subject: B.1 schema + migration for featured column
content.description: Add featured boolean default false on extensions table; generate Drizzle migration; add composite index (featured, publishedAt DESC). Commit: feat(p?-db): add featured column to extensions

content.subject: B.2 repo findFeatured + listSelect tagline
content.description: Extend listSelect with tagline/taglineZh; rewrite findFeatured to prefer featured=true with fallback to top-downloaded; integration test. Commit: feat(p?-repo): findFeatured prefers featured rows

content.subject: B.3 ExtFeatureHero + endpoint + home wiring
content.description: Create /api/internal/featured.get.ts; build ExtFeatureHero component reusing ExtHero + InstallCommand; wire into app/pages/index.vue with fallback for empty state; component test. Commit: feat(p?-ui): ExtFeatureHero on home

content.subject: A top bar slim-down + sidebar primary nav
content.description: Drop inline nav from TopBar; ghost search; 64px header in both layouts; unforce mcp-panorama collapse; add Explore section to Sidebar with 4 nav links; update Sidebar test. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): move primary nav to sidebar, ghost search, 64px header

content.subject: C.1 FuncCategoryPicker component
content.description: New filter component mirroring DeptPicker's expandedPath + flatten() pattern; reads/writes funcCat/subCat/l2 via useFilters; slot into FilterBar; component test. Commit: feat(p?-filters): FuncCategoryPicker

content.subject: C.2 drop categories section from sidebar
content.description: Delete Sidebar.vue lines 122-208 plus related state/imports; rewrite Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts to drop Categories assertions. Commit: refactor(p?-layout): drop categories section from sidebar

content.subject: D.1 accent restraint pass
content.description: Swap sidebar active states to left-rule + neutral ink; neutralize secondary filter pickers; keep ScopePills accent; remove fallback hero gradient; smoke-test all three themes. Commit: refactor(p?-ui): restrain accent usage on secondary surfaces

content.subject: D.2 docs: accent convention
content.description: Add item 11 to CLAUDE.md Locked Product Decisions; record convention in docs/plan.md. Commit: docs: lock accent-usage convention

content.text: Total accent hits 113 → 106; more importantly, the high-traffic surfaces (sidebar active state, filter trigger pills) are now neutralized so per-viewport accent moments drop sharply. Committing D.1.

input.command:
git add -A && git diff --stat HEAD && echo "---" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(p24-elegance-ui): restrain accent usage on secondary surfaces

Two coordinated swaps so the home featured hero, primary CTAs, and
focus rings stay as the accent moments, while crowded chrome stops
competing for attention:

- Sidebar active state: tinted fill + accent text → left-rule border +
  neutral ink + bold weight. The accent strip carries the "you're here"
  signal without saturating the whole row. Applies to primary nav links
  (active route) and Browse type pills (active category).

- Filter trigger pills (Dept, FuncCategory, Creator, Publisher,
  TagDrawer): active state goes from `border-accent/40 bg-accent/10
  text-accent` to `border-ink/35 bg-card text-ink font-semibold`. The
  row still reads as "filter applied" via weight + border tone, just
  without the accent fill. Inner popover row selections keep their
  accent text — those only show during a focused interaction.

ScopePills (the primary filter) keeps its existing ink-fill active
state; it never used accent fill, so no change there. Featured hero,
extension detail CTA, sign-in/up flows, UI primitive focus rings — all
intentionally untouched.

Accent hits go from 113 → 106 globally, but the meaningful drop is
per-viewport: home, /extensions, and the sidebar now each have one or
two intentional accent moments instead of six to ten.
EOF
)"

file.content:
# AgentCenter (Nuxt)

Marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins) with internal-enterprise features (departments, scope, tags, collections) and a companion CLI for actual machine-side install. Bilingual (EN/ZH) from day one.

This is a rewrite of the original Next.js implementation in Nuxt 4. The product surface and the public `/api/v1` contract are preserved verbatim so the existing CLI keeps working.

See `docs/plan.md` for the full implementation plan, milestone schedule, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries, and detailed commit/PR rules.

## Tech stack

- TypeScript, Nuxt 4 (Nitro server, Vue 3, Vite), Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn-vue + Reka UI
- Bun for local dev, scripts, and CLI build; Node runtime in production
- PostgreSQL (Supabase by default; any Postgres via `DATABASE_URL`) with Drizzle ORM (`drizzle-orm/postgres-js`)
- Better Auth (cookie sessions, Drizzle adapter); CLI uses device-code flow on the same `sessions` table
- Supabase Storage for bundle storage (default); R2 via S3 SDK supported as alternative
- Inngest for background jobs (scan, index, publish)
- `@nuxtjs/i18n` with `strategy: 'prefix'`; locales `en` (default) + `zh`, always-prefixed URLs
- Fonts via `@nuxt/fonts`: Inter (UI), Fraunces (display, italic), JetBrains Mono (tags/counts)
- Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm` for search; Meilisearch deferred
- vee-validate + Zod for forms
- markdown-it + DOMPurify for README rendering (server-side)
- Pinia (`@pinia/nuxt`) only where cross-component client state is needed

## Locked product decisions

These mirror the original v1 product decisions. Same numbering; do not revisit inside a normal PR. If a PR needs to change one, update this section and `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark + Mono Clean. Selectable via the TopBar theme picker; persisted in a `theme` cookie.
3. **Filter UI**: Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only.
4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm.
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.
10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.

Nuxt-specific technical decisions (deployment target, auth choice, DB driver, storage default, public API contract, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries) live in `docs/plan.md` so they only have to be maintained in one place.

## Workflow

- **Commit per coherent unit** — usually one or several commits per phase, split where natural (schema vs. consumers, refactor vs. feature, deps vs. first use). Conventional Commits with phase scope: `feat(p2-db): add extension drizzle schema`. See `docs/plan.md` §13 for granularity calibration and the per-commit vs. per-PR validate split.
- **Pause at each phase boundary** for an explicit human checkpoint before starting the next phase. Never run `git push` without an explicit request.
- **No `--amend` once pushed, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.
- **Breaking changes to `/api/v1`** require `!` after the scope and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
- Always work on a feature branch and open a PR — never commit to `main` directly.
- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.
- **No agent-attribution noise in commits or PRs.** Do not add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: Claude …`) to commit messages, and do not append the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" footer to PR descriptions. The branch already records authorship and the content speaks for itself.

## Project structure

See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree.

- `app/pages/...` — file-based routes (locale-prefixed via `@nuxtjs/i18n`)
- `app/components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish,profile}` — Vue components
- `app/composables/`, `app/middleware/` — client state + route guards
- `server/api/v1/...` — public registry API consumed by the CLI (frozen contract)
- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.
11. **Accent usage**: `--color-accent` is reserved for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action. Secondary active states (sidebar nav, filter trigger pills, non-primary picker chips) use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight, not tinted fills. The home should carry at most two accent moments per viewport; the goal is "one intentional accent moment" wherever practical. Auth pages, error states, and UI primitive focus rings are exempt.

- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

lines: 8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm.
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.

+11. **Accent usage**: `--color-accent` is reserved for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action. Secondary active states (sidebar nav, filter trigger pills, non-primary picker chips) use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight, not tinted fills. The home should carry at most two accent moments per viewport; the goal is "one intentional accent moment" wherever practical. Auth pages, error states, and UI primitive focus rings are exempt.

Nuxt-specific technical decisions (deployment target, auth choice, DB driver, storage default, public API contract, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries) live in `docs/plan.md` so they only have to be maintained in one place.

input.new_string:
`ThemeSwitch` is a dropdown (sun / moon / contrast icons + preview tiles per option) that writes the `theme` cookie. `useTheme` reads the cookie via `useCookie` and applies the matching class (`""` for ivory, `dark`, `mono`) to `<html>` via `useHead` — serialised into the SSR HTML so the initial paint already carries the correct theme, no flash on hydrate. Fonts loaded via `@nuxt/fonts` and wired into the CSS variables.

### Accent usage convention

`--color-accent` is the only chromatic token in the editorial palette, so it has to be spent carefully. Locked decision #11 (in `CLAUDE.md`) governs where it appears.

**Use accent for:**
- Primary CTAs (Install on the detail page, the home hero's "View extension" arrow).
- Focus rings on form inputs and interactive controls.
- The home featured hero (left-rule + CTA arrow only).
- The Extension detail page's install button.

**Do NOT use accent for:**
- Sidebar active state — use a 2px left rule + neutral ink + bold weight.
- Filter trigger pills (Dept, FuncCategory, Creator, Publisher, TagDrawer) when applied — use `border-(--color-ink)/35 + font-semibold` instead of tinted fill.
- Non-primary popover row selections — text-only emphasis is fine.

**Exempt (intentionally accent-heavy):**
- Auth flows (sign-in, sign-up, CLI device auth, onboarding) — these screens are essentially landing pages with one CTA each.
- UI primitive focus rings (`Button`, `Input`, `Checkbox`, `SelectTrigger`, `Textarea`) — accent focus is a system signal, not chrome.
- Error boundaries — the retry CTA carries the accent fill.

Rule of thumb: a viewport should carry **at most two** intentional accent moments. The home should typically have **one** (the featured hero or the fallback CTA). When introducing a new accent hit, ask whether it's earning attention or just adding noise — if removing it doesn't degrade comprehension, remove it.

---

## 6. Auth & access model

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(p24): elegance pass — featured hero, slim top bar, taxonomy → filter row, accent restraint" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Four-phase layout pass to raise the elegance ceiling without touching the design system: theme palette, fonts, and CSS variables are unchanged. Single PR, 8 commits in B → A → C → D order so the schema migration lands first and stands alone.

## Phases

### B — Featured home hero (3 commits)
- Schema: new `featured: boolean default false` column on `extensions` with a composite `(featured, published_at DESC)` index. Migration `0005_gifted_quasar.sql`.
- Repo: `listSelect` picks up `tagline` / `taglineZh`; `findFeatured` now prefers `featured = true` (newest publishedAt wins among ties) and falls back to top-downloaded so the home is never empty.
- UI: new `ExtFeatureHero.vue` — eyebrow + serif headline + tagline + dept trail + `agentcenter install <slug>` line + "View extension" arrow. Reuses `InstallCommand` and `ExtHero`'s typography. New `/api/internal/featured` endpoint with 60s `s-maxage`. Home page renders the featured hero when one exists, otherwise a slim accent-free fallback.

### A — Top bar slim-down + sidebar primary nav (1 commit)
- `TopBar` drops its four inline nav links and adopts a ghost search input (borderless until focus reveals border + ring).
- New "Explore" section at the top of the sidebar with lucide-icon nav links to Extensions / MCP Panorama / Publish, plus a disabled Docs placeholder.
- Header grows 52px → 64px in both layouts.
- `mcp-panorama` layout gains the global aside slot (collapsed by default so its own `LayerSidebar` keeps the gutter), keeping primary nav reachable via the menu toggle.

### C — Taxonomy → filter row (2 commits)
- New `FuncCategoryPicker.vue` mirroring `DeptPicker`'s `expandedPath` + `flatten()` pattern over `FUNC_TAXONOMY`'s three levels. Reads/writes `funcCat` / `subCat` / `l2` through the existing `useFilters` composable — URL contract unchanged so saved/shared links keep working.
- Sidebar loses its 90-line Categories tree (`Sidebar.vue` -127 lines) and the related state. Remaining sections: Explore → Browse → Collections.

### D — Accent restraint (2 commits)
- Sidebar active states: tinted fill → 2px left rule + neutral ink + bold weight.
- Filter trigger pills (Dept / FuncCategory / Creator / Publisher / TagDrawer): accent fill on the "applied" state → `border-(--color-ink)/35 font-semibold`. Inner popover row selections keep accent text (focused interaction).
- `CLAUDE.md` gains locked decision #11 codifying the convention; `docs/plan.md` Theming section picks up a longer do/don't/exempt breakdown with the "at most two accent moments per viewport" budget.

## Notes

- Phase scope: `p24-elegance-*` (continuing from p21–p23 in plan.md).
- `/api/v1` contract: untouched — the new `featured` column is not exposed via the public API. The v1 list endpoint maps fields explicitly rather than spreading.
- No new dependencies, no new color tokens, no new fonts.
- Two new i18n keys: `sidebar.explore` and `filters.funcCat.{label,all}` (en + zh).

## Test plan

- [ ] `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + 247 unit tests) — green locally
- [ ] `bun run test:integration` (47 tests including new `findFeatured` coverage) — green locally
- [ ] Apply migration: `bun run db:migrate`
- [ ] Smoke the home with `UPDATE extensions SET featured = true WHERE slug = '<any-published-slug>';` — featured hero renders; clear the flag and confirm the slim fallback hero takes over
- [ ] Browse `/extensions?funcCat=workTask&subCat=systemDesign&l2=archDesign` — picker trigger reads "Function: Work Task / System Design / Architecture Design"; opening the popover shows the right expansion state; selecting another L2 updates the URL and the list
- [ ] Open `/mcp-panorama`, click the menu toggle — global sidebar reveals primary nav; `LayerSidebar` still functional
- [ ] Switch through Ivory / Dark / Mono Clean themes — accent restraint reads correctly in all three
- [ ] Search input on `/` — focus reveals border + accent ring; blur hides the border
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

**Assert the locale-prefixed route explicitly in the link test.**

This assertion only checks for `"/extensions/web-search"` as a substring, so it can pass even if the locale prefix is missing. Tighten it to validate prefixed URLs.

<details>
<summary>Proposed test hardening</summary>

```diff
-    const links = wrapper.findAll("a").map((a) => a.attributes("href"))
-    expect(links.some((href) => href?.includes("/extensions/web-search"))).toBe(true)
+    const links = wrapper.findAll("a").map((a) => a.attributes("href") ?? "")
+    expect(links.some((href) => /^\/(en|zh)\/extensions\/web-search$/.test(href))).toBe(true)

As per coding guidelines, `app/pages/**/*.vue` routes must use always-prefixed URLs for `en` and `zh`.

The test currently only checks for "/extensions/web-search" which allows missing
locale prefixes; update the assertion in the "links to the locale-prefixed
detail page" test for ExtFeatureHero so it explicitly expects a locale-prefixed
URL. Locate the test that mounts ExtFeatureHero via mountSuspended with
fixture({ slug: "web-search" }) and change the final expect to assert that one
of the anchor hrefs matches a locale-prefixed path (e.g. startsWith or matches
/^\/(en|zh)\/extensions\/web-search/ or uses the test's current locale like
`/${i18nLocale}/extensions/web-search`) to ensure URLs always include the en/zh
prefix.
```

**Fix locale-aware compact number formatting in download count displays.**

`formatCount` hardcodes the English `k` suffix, causing Chinese users to see inconsistent notation. Use `Intl.NumberFormat(..., { notation: "compact" })` to respect locale (Chinese will display `万` instead of `k`).

**Note:** This function is duplicated identically in ExtHero.vue and ExtCard.vue. Consider extracting to a shared composable in `app/composables/` to avoid maintenance issues across all three components.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix for ExtFeatureHero.vue</summary>

```diff
-function formatCount(n: number): string {
-  if (n < 1000) return String(n)
-  return `${(n / 1000).toFixed(n >= 100000 ? 0 : 1)}k`
-}

**Add stable secondary sort keys to prevent non-deterministic hero selection.**

When multiple extensions share the same `publishedAt` (curated path) or `downloadsCount` (fallback path), the `.limit(1)` query returns non-deterministic results across requests. Add secondary and tertiary sort keys to guarantee consistent selection.

<details>
<summary>Suggested diff</summary>

```diff
-    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt))
+    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
...
-    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
+    .orderBy(
+      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
+      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
+      desc(extensions.id),
+    )
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**

```suggestion
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(
      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
      desc(extensions.id),
    )
    .limit(1)
```

</details>

pick a single "hero" extension use .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt)) and
.orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount)) with .limit(1), which is
non-deterministic when ties occur; update both the curated path (the query
returning curated) and the fallback path (the query building fallback from

after the primary desc sort so results are consistently ordered; keep the

content.text:
CodeRabbit posted 5 inline comments. Summary:

| # | File:line | Severity | Issue |
|---|-----------|----------|-------|
| 1 | `ExtFeatureHero.nuxt.test.ts:70` | 🟡 Minor | Link assertion uses substring match — could pass even if the locale prefix is missing. Tighten to `/^\/(en\|zh)\/…/` regex. |
| 2 | `ExtFeatureHero.vue:42` | 🟡 Minor | `formatCount` hardcodes English `k` suffix; zh users should see 万. Same code is duplicated in `ExtHero.vue` + `ExtCard.vue` — suggests extracting to a composable. |
| 3 | `FuncCategoryPicker.vue:158,194` | 🟠 Major | The "All functions" chip uses `bg-accent/10` and the active row uses `text-accent` — violates the accent-restraint convention I locked in D.2. |
| 4 | `Sidebar.vue:113,142,178` | 🟡 Minor | Sidebar active items use `border-l-(--color-accent)` and the collection dot uses `bg-(--color-accent)` — same convention violation. |
| 5 | `server/repositories/extensions.ts:137-148` | (suggestion) | `findFeatured` ORDER BY is non-deterministic on ties — add `desc(extensions.id)` as secondary key for both curated and fallback paths. |

My take on each:

- **#1, #4, #5: accept.** These are clean wins — #1 enforces the locked locale rule, #4 fixes my own self-contradiction (I codified "no accent in secondary surfaces" then shipped accent in the sidebar left rule), #5 is a real correctness bug.
- **#3: accept and broaden.** CodeRabbit is right that line 158 (chip with `bg-accent/10`) violates my own convention. Line 194 (row `text-accent`) is debatable — my D.1 commit message reserved it as "fine for focused interactions" but my docs/plan.md DON'T list reads the opposite way. The cleaner resolution is to fix the contradiction in the docs **and** strip accent from inner popover rows so FuncCategoryPicker, DeptPicker, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker are uniform.
- **#2: skip.** `formatCount` duplication and Chinese locale support is real, but the pattern pre-dates this PR (lives in `ExtHero.vue` and `ExtCard.vue` too) and fixing it properly means extracting a composable and touching all three. That's a focused follow-up PR, not scope creep on an elegance pass.

Total work if you want me to address #1, #3, #4, #5: ~15 lines across 5–6 files plus a docs/plan.md tightening. Probably 2 commits (`fix(p24-elegance-ui): apply accent restraint to ...` and `fix(p24-elegance-repo): deterministic findFeatured ordering`).

Want me to push those fixes? Or address only a subset?

lastPrompt: check the code reviews on the PR

content.text: Tightening the convention text in docs/plan.md so the "text-only emphasis" clause isn't ambiguous.

input.old_string:
**Do NOT use accent for:**
- Sidebar active state — use a 2px left rule + neutral ink + bold weight.
- Filter trigger pills (Dept, FuncCategory, Creator, Publisher, TagDrawer) when applied — use `border-(--color-ink)/35 + font-semibold` instead of tinted fill.
- Non-primary popover row selections — text-only emphasis is fine.
input.new_string:
**Do NOT use accent for:**
- Sidebar active state — use a 2px left rule in `--color-ink`, plus `bg-(--color-sidebar)/60` and `font-semibold`. No tinted fills, no accent on the rule itself.
- Filter trigger pills (Dept, FuncCategory, Creator, Publisher, TagDrawer) when applied — use `border-(--color-ink)/35 + font-semibold` instead of tinted fill.
- Inner popover chips and row selections — neutral ink + `font-semibold`. The popover is itself a focused interaction; the selected row reads as "current" through weight without needing chromatic emphasis.
- Decorative dots and bullets in the sidebar — use `bg-(--color-ink-muted)`.

fix(p24-elegance-ui): apply accent restraint to popovers + sidebar rule

Address CodeRabbit review on PR #23. Four corrections so the
just-locked accent-usage convention is honored end-to-end instead of
half-applied:

- Sidebar active items: left rule shifts from --color-accent to
  --color-ink (still 2px, still visible against bg-sidebar/60). The
  collection placeholder dot drops from --color-accent to
  --color-ink-muted — it's decorative, not signal.

- Inner popover row selections (DeptPicker, FuncCategoryPicker,
  CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker): swap text-(--color-accent) +
  font-medium for text-(--color-ink) + font-semibold. The popover is
  itself a focused interaction; the active row reads through weight,
  not color.

- DeptPicker Mine/Org/All chips and FuncCategoryPicker "All functions"
  chip: drop the bg-(--color-accent)/10 tint when active. Use
  border-(--color-ink)/35 + font-semibold to match the trigger pills
  D.1 already neutralized.

- ExtFeatureHero test: tighten the locale-prefix assertion from a
  substring check to /^\/(en|zh)\/extensions\/web-search$/, honoring
  CLAUDE.md locked decision #5 (locale URLs always prefixed).

docs/plan.md gets a sharper Do-Not list — the previous "text-only
emphasis is fine" was ambiguous enough to license accent text inside
popovers, which contradicted the rest of the convention. Now: no accent
in sidebar rule, no accent in popover selections, no accent on
decorative dots. The rule is enforceable on sight.
EOF
)"

content.text: Now #5 — deterministic ORDER BY in `findFeatured`.

input.old_string:
export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

  return fallback ?? null
}
toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"

import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import {
  buildExtensionOrder,
  buildExtensionWhere,
} from "~~/shared/search/query"
import {
  PAGE_SIZE,
  pageOffset,
  type Filters,
} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

import type { Transactable } from "./types"

// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
// PGlite-backed integration test DB.

// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
// rename.
const listSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const detailSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
  permissions: extensions.permissions,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
  visibility: extensions.visibility,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const relatedSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
}

export async function findManyForList(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
) {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)

  return db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(...order)
    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
}

export async function countFiltered(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
): Promise<number> {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const [row] = await db
    .select({ count: count() })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(where)
  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
}

export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(p24-elegance-repo): deterministic ordering in findFeatured

Both branches of findFeatured cap at one row, so a tie on the primary
sort key silently lets Postgres pick whichever it likes — the home hero
would then flap between requests when two curated rows share a
publishedAt, or two published rows share a downloadsCount.

Add stable tiebreakers:
- Curated path: orderBy(desc(publishedAt), desc(id))
- Fallback path: orderBy(desc(downloadsCount), desc(publishedAt), desc(id))

id is the primary key, so it's guaranteed unique and stable. desc()
keeps "newest wins" semantics on the publishedAt tiebreaker in the
fallback chain.

Two integration tests cover the ties — one for each path — confirming
the home picks the same row across requests.
EOF
)"

content.text:
Two fix commits pushed to PR #23:

- `047e601 fix(p24-elegance-ui): apply accent restraint to popovers + sidebar rule` — addresses #1 (locale-prefix test assertion), #3 (FuncCategoryPicker chip + row, plus matching cleanup in DeptPicker / CreatorPicker / PublisherPicker so all four pickers are consistent), #4 (Sidebar left rule → ink, collection dot → ink-muted), and sharpens the docs/plan.md convention so the ambiguity that caused this drift can't recur.
- `468418d fix(p24-elegance-repo): deterministic ordering in findFeatured` — addresses #5, adds `desc(id)` tiebreakers to both query paths, plus two integration tests that pin the behavior under ties.

Validate (lint + 247 unit) + 49 integration tests green locally. #2 (locale-aware `formatCount`) intentionally skipped — pre-existing duplication across three components; cleaner as a focused follow-up PR.

content.text:
Re-entered plan mode. The previous plan file describes the four-phase elegance pass that just shipped (PR #23). This new request is a different task — it partially reverts two of those decisions (primary nav placement, function-type prominence). I'll overwrite the plan file once we agree on shape.

**My take on the design:**

You're right on both counts, and they're separable.

- **Primary nav → top bar:** The Phase A move to sidebar was driven by aesthetic restraint, but it sacrificed convention. Discoverability for first-time users (and the conventional muscle memory of returning users) is worth more than the "quieter top bar" win. Net positive.
- **Function types → sidebar:** Bigger judgment call. Phase C's logic was "taxonomy is refinement, so it belongs in the filter drawer." But for enterprise users who *navigate by domain*, the taxonomy IS the primary information architecture, not a filter on a flat list. Putting it back in the sidebar makes it a first-class navigation surface. Net positive for your audience.

The interesting follow-on question is what happens to `FuncCategoryPicker` (the filter-row picker I just built in Phase C.1) once the sidebar carries the same taxonomy. I see three options worth considering — let me ask before planning.

questions.question: What should happen to FuncCategoryPicker in the filter row?

options.label: Remove it — sidebar is canonical
options.description: Drop FuncCategoryPicker from FilterBar entirely. One place to filter by function (the sidebar tree). Cleaner mental model. Loses the ability to drill into function from a deep URL without scrolling the sidebar.

options.label: Keep both — sidebar + filter row
options.description: Sidebar tree is the navigation entry point; filter row picker is a quick refinement when already on /extensions. Both write the same URL params, both stay in sync. More surfaces to maintain.

options.label: Keep only on mobile / collapsed sidebar
options.description: When the sidebar is collapsed (mobile, narrow screens), surface FuncCategoryPicker in the filter row as the fallback entry point. On desktop with sidebar open, hide it. Most adaptive but most complex.

questions.question: How should the sidebar look once the function tree is back?

options.label: Function types as the main section, Browse + Collections below
options.description: Function tree gets the most real estate, expanded by default for the first funcCat. Browse (type pills: Skills / MCP / Slash / Plugins) sits below as a quick filter. Collections at the bottom. Matches the 'enterprise navigates by domain' framing.

options.label: Restore the pre-refactor three-section layout
options.description: Browse → Function types → Collections, exactly as it was before Phase C. Familiar, no surprises. Browse takes the top of the sidebar (less prominence for function types).

options.label: Two columns: Browse pills on top, function tree below
options.description: Browse becomes a horizontal row of pill chips at the top of the sidebar (compact); function tree fills the remaining vertical space. Maximum room for the taxonomy.

questions.question: Top bar after restoring primary nav — keep ghost search and 64px height?

options.label: Keep both (recommended)
options.description: Ghost search and 64px header were independent wins. Restoring nav doesn't require giving them up — the top bar becomes: hamburger · brand · ghost search · 4 nav links · theme · locale · user. Roomier than the original.

options.label: Revert to original 52px + bordered search
options.description: Full revert of Phase A. Back to the cramped 52px top bar with a bordered search input. Loses the 'roomier header' aesthetic gain.

options.label: Keep 64px, revert ghost search
options.description: Header stays roomy but search gets its solid border back — more discoverable when not focused.

answers.How should the sidebar look once the fun: I think the browse should be choose first, then choose function types. and we don't need the "all extensions" tab, we can choose a type of extension then check the function types. what do you think?

content.text:
On the sequencing — quick reaction to "what do you think?":

I read your description as **independent filters that compose, visually sequential**: Browse sits above Function Types in the sidebar (signaling the typical pick-a-type-then-narrow flow), but functionally the two URL params are independent — picking a function type without first picking a type still works, so `?funcCat=workTask` shared URLs aren't broken. Dropping "All Extensions" is the right move; the implicit-all-when-nothing-selected pattern is cleaner, and clicking an already-active type pill toggles it off. I'll bring back the same pattern for the function-types tree (no "All" item at the top — toggle-to-clear).

Writing the plan.

input.content:
# Layout revision — primary nav back to top bar, function types back to sidebar

## Context

PR #23 (the four-phase elegance pass) shipped two moves that the enterprise audience needs reversed:

1. **Primary nav lives in the sidebar** (Phase A) — discoverability suffers; conventional muscle memory expects nav in the top bar.
2. **Function types live behind a popover in the filter row** (Phase C) — enterprise users navigate primarily by functional domain (workTask → systemDesign → archDesign). Burying that taxonomy in a Popover demotes it from primary information architecture to secondary refinement.

This revision restores both surfaces to where they were before #23 but **keeps the elegance wins that were independent** of those decisions: ghost search, 64px header, featured home hero, accent restraint, the FuncCategoryPicker pattern itself (deleted but the work informed the sidebar tree's interaction model).

User selections (this session):
- FuncCategoryPicker: **remove from filter row** (sidebar is canonical, single source of truth for function-type navigation).
- Sidebar layout: **Browse first, then Function Types, then Collections.** No "All Extensions" item in Browse — clicking an active type pill toggles it off, the implicit-all state covers "no filter".
- Top bar: **keep ghost search + 64px height.** Restoring nav doesn't undo those independent wins.

## Locked decisions for this revision

1. **Phase token**: `p25-restore-nav`. Continues the numbering (p21–p24 are taken).
2. **Delivery**: single feature branch off `main` *after* #23 merges. **One PR, 3 commits** (top bar restore, sidebar restore + Browse trim, FuncCategoryPicker delete). Same one-PR-many-commits pattern as #23 — user already opted into bundling.
3. **Filter composition**: Browse (`category`) and Function Types (`funcCat` / `subCat` / `l2`) remain **independent URL params**. The sidebar's visual ordering (Browse on top, Function Types below) communicates the typical sequence without enforcing it — `?funcCat=workTask` alone still works, `?category=skills` alone still works, and they compose freely.
4. **No "All" items**: Drop both "All Extensions" from Browse and any "All" item from the function-types tree. Toggle-to-clear is the universal pattern; an explicit "All" duplicates that affordance and adds chrome.
5. **mcp-panorama layout**: revert to its pre-#23 shape (no global aside slot). With primary nav restored to the TopBar, the panorama page no longer needs the global sidebar for nav reachability, and the page-owned LayerSidebar gets its full gutter back.
6. **Accent restraint convention** (CLAUDE.md decision #11) still applies. The restored function-types tree should follow it from the start — no `bg-(--color-accent)/10` on active rows; use left-rule + ink + bold, matching the sidebar's primary nav active state shipped in #23's `047e601`.

## Files modified

### Restored / changed
- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — re-add the inline `<nav>` block with the four primary nav links (Explore, MCP Panorama, Publish, Docs-as-disabled-span). Keep the existing ghost search input and the 64px-friendly spacing — those are independent wins.
- `app/components/layout/Sidebar.vue` — remove the primary-nav "Explore" section (added in #23 Phase A). Restore the function-types tree between Browse and Collections, mirroring the pre-#23 structure but with the accent-restraint convention applied (left-rule on active, no tinted fills). Drop the "All Extensions" item from `BROWSE_ITEMS`.
- `app/components/layout/Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts` — drop the four primary-nav-in-sidebar assertions (added in #23 Phase A). Re-add coverage for the function-types tree presence + default-expanded `workTask` + the negative assertion that hidden categories stay collapsed.
- `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove the `<FuncCategoryPicker />` slot added in #23 Phase C.
- `app/layouts/mcp-panorama.vue` — revert to no-global-aside layout (delete the `<div class="flex flex-1 min-h-0">…<aside>…</aside>` wrapper added in Phase A); keep the 64px header.
- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` — remove `sidebar.explore` (no longer used) and `filters.funcCat.{label,all}` (FuncCategoryPicker is being deleted). All other keys stay.

### Deleted
- `app/components/filters/FuncCategoryPicker.vue` — superseded by the sidebar tree.
- `app/components/filters/FuncCategoryPicker.nuxt.test.ts` — paired test, deleted with the component.

### Untouched (independent wins from #23, intentionally preserved)
- `app/components/extension/ExtFeatureHero.vue` + endpoint + schema — Phase B stays in full.
- `app/layouts/default.vue` — 64px header stays.
- Ghost search input in TopBar — stays.
- All Phase D accent-restraint changes in `Sidebar.vue` (left-rule on active using `--color-ink`), `Dept/Creator/Publisher Picker` neutralization, `docs/plan.md` + `CLAUDE.md` convention.
- `server/repositories/extensions.ts` — featured-row deterministic ordering stays.
- `shared/db/schema/extension.ts` + migration — `featured` column stays.

## Reused

toolUseResult.content:
# Layout revision — primary nav back to top bar, function types back to sidebar

## Context

PR #23 (the four-phase elegance pass) shipped two moves that the enterprise audience needs reversed:

1. **Primary nav lives in the sidebar** (Phase A) — discoverability suffers; conventional muscle memory expects nav in the top bar.
2. **Function types live behind a popover in the filter row** (Phase C) — enterprise users navigate primarily by functional domain (workTask → systemDesign → archDesign). Burying that taxonomy in a Popover demotes it from primary information architecture to secondary refinement.

This revision restores both surfaces to where they were before #23 but **keeps the elegance wins that were independent** of those decisions: ghost search, 64px header, featured home hero, accent restraint, the FuncCategoryPicker pattern itself (deleted but the work informed the sidebar tree's interaction model).

User selections (this session):
- FuncCategoryPicker: **remove from filter row** (sidebar is canonical, single source of truth for function-type navigation).
- Sidebar layout: **Browse first, then Function Types, then Collections.** No "All Extensions" item in Browse — clicking an active type pill toggles it off, the implicit-all state covers "no filter".
- Top bar: **keep ghost search + 64px height.** Restoring nav doesn't undo those independent wins.

## Locked decisions for this revision

1. **Phase token**: `p25-restore-nav`. Continues the numbering (p21–p24 are taken).
2. **Delivery**: single feature branch off `main` *after* #23 merges. **One PR, 3 commits** (top bar restore, sidebar restore + Browse trim, FuncCategoryPicker delete). Same one-PR-many-commits pattern as #23 — user already opted into bundling.
3. **Filter composition**: Browse (`category`) and Function Types (`funcCat` / `subCat` / `l2`) remain **independent URL params**. The sidebar's visual ordering (Browse on top, Function Types below) communicates the typical sequence without enforcing it — `?funcCat=workTask` alone still works, `?category=skills` alone still works, and they compose freely.
4. **No "All" items**: Drop both "All Extensions" from Browse and any "All" item from the function-types tree. Toggle-to-clear is the universal pattern; an explicit "All" duplicates that affordance and adds chrome.
5. **mcp-panorama layout**: revert to its pre-#23 shape (no global aside slot). With primary nav restored to the TopBar, the panorama page no longer needs the global sidebar for nav reachability, and the page-owned LayerSidebar gets its full gutter back.
6. **Accent restraint convention** (CLAUDE.md decision #11) still applies. The restored function-types tree should follow it from the start — no `bg-(--color-accent)/10` on active rows; use left-rule + ink + bold, matching the sidebar's primary nav active state shipped in #23's `047e601`.

## Files modified

### Restored / changed
- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — re-add the inline `<nav>` block with the four primary nav links (Explore, MCP Panorama, Publish, Docs-as-disabled-span). Keep the existing ghost search input and the 64px-friendly spacing — those are independent wins.
- `app/components/layout/Sidebar.vue` — remove the primary-nav "Explore" section (added in #23 Phase A). Restore the function-types tree between Browse and Collections, mirroring the pre-#23 structure but with the accent-restraint convention applied (left-rule on active, no tinted fills). Drop the "All Extensions" item from `BROWSE_ITEMS`.
- `app/components/layout/Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts` — drop the four primary-nav-in-sidebar assertions (added in #23 Phase A). Re-add coverage for the function-types tree presence + default-expanded `workTask` + the negative assertion that hidden categories stay collapsed.
- `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove the `<FuncCategoryPicker />` slot added in #23 Phase C.
- `app/layouts/mcp-panorama.vue` — revert to no-global-aside layout (delete the `<div class="flex flex-1 min-h-0">…<aside>…</aside>` wrapper added in Phase A); keep the 64px header.
- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` — remove `sidebar.explore` (no longer used) and `filters.funcCat.{label,all}` (FuncCategoryPicker is being deleted). All other keys stay.

### Deleted
- `app/components/filters/FuncCategoryPicker.vue` — superseded by the sidebar tree.
- `app/components/filters/FuncCategoryPicker.nuxt.test.ts` — paired test, deleted with the component.

### Untouched (independent wins from #23, intentionally preserved)
- `app/components/extension/ExtFeatureHero.vue` + endpoint + schema — Phase B stays in full.
- `app/layouts/default.vue` — 64px header stays.
- Ghost search input in TopBar — stays.
- All Phase D accent-restraint changes in `Sidebar.vue` (left-rule on active using `--color-ink`), `Dept/Creator/Publisher Picker` neutralization, `docs/plan.md` + `CLAUDE.md` convention.
- `server/repositories/extensions.ts` — featured-row deterministic ordering stays.
- `shared/db/schema/extension.ts` + migration — `featured` column stays.

## Reused

- `FUNC_TAXONOMY` and `FUNC_CAT_COLORS` from `shared/taxonomy.ts` — same data structure the deleted FuncCategoryPicker consumed.
- Pre-#23 sidebar tree implementation pattern (`activeFuncCat` / `activeSubCat` / `activeL2` computeds, `expansion.funcCats` / `expansion.l1` reactive state, `toggleFuncCat` / `toggleL1` handlers, `buildHref` URL writer) — recoverable from `git show 8b12227:app/components/layout/Sidebar.vue` (the commit at the top of Phase B, immediately before Phase A removed it). `buildHref` itself is still in the current Sidebar (used by Browse), so it just needs new callers.
- Sidebar active-state visual treatment from #23's `047e601`: `border-l-2 border-(--color-ink) bg-(--color-sidebar)/60 font-semibold`. Apply this to function-tree rows when active, not the original `bg-accent/10 text-accent`.

## Commits

1. `refactor(p25-restore-nav-topbar): restore inline primary nav in TopBar`
   - TopBar regains the four nav links (Explore / MCP Panorama / Publish / Docs disabled).
   - Keeps ghost search + roomier spacing.
2. `refactor(p25-restore-nav-sidebar): restore function-types tree, drop "All Extensions"`
   - Sidebar loses the Explore section, gains the function-types tree (accent-restrained from the start).
   - `BROWSE_ITEMS` loses `all`.
   - `Sidebar.nuxt.test.ts` rewritten for the new structure.
   - `mcp-panorama.vue` reverts the aside addition.
   - i18n: drop `sidebar.explore`.
3. `chore(p25-restore-nav): delete FuncCategoryPicker (superseded by sidebar tree)`
   - Delete component + test.
   - Remove `<FuncCategoryPicker />` from FilterBar.
   - i18n: drop `filters.funcCat.*`.

## Risks

- **`mcp-panorama` layout revert** — the page's own `LayerSidebar` should fill the gutter again with no global aside competing. Verify visually that the page hasn't started depending on the aside being present (it shouldn't have — Phase A added it specifically because nav had nowhere else to go).
- **URL contract preserved** — `?funcCat=…&subCat=…&l2=…` continues to round-trip cleanly. The sidebar tree writes those params through the same `buildHref` it always did, and `useFilters`/`shared/validators/filters.ts` already understand them.
- **No "All Extensions" item** — verify the toggle-to-clear UX on the active type pill in BROWSE_ITEMS feels obvious. The pattern is already in place at `Sidebar.vue` `:to="buildHref({ category: item.key === activeCategory ? null : … })"` line 164.
- **i18n keys removed** — quick grep before deleting: `sidebar.explore` is only referenced by the Sidebar's explore section; `filters.funcCat.*` is only referenced by FuncCategoryPicker. Both should be dead after this revision.
- **PR #23 not yet merged** — open this revision after #23 merges, off a fresh branch from `main`. If #23 is still under review and the user wants this folded in instead, append the three commits to `feat/layout-elegance-pass` and update the PR description.

## Verification

End-to-end after all three commits:

- `bun run validate` — lint + typecheck + 247 unit tests (the Sidebar test rewrite changes shape but count holds; the FuncCategoryPicker test deletion drops 5).
- `bun run test:integration` — 49 tests, no change expected (no repository or server changes).
- Manual:
  - `/` — featured hero unchanged.
  - TopBar carries the four inline nav links again, plus ghost search + 64px height.
  - Sidebar shows three sections in order: Browse (4 items, no "All Extensions") → Function Types (tree with the same expand/collapse + chevrons + color swatches as before #23) → Collections.
  - Click a type pill (e.g. Skills) → URL gets `?category=skills`; click again → URL clears. Same toggle behavior.
  - Click a function-type leaf (e.g. workTask → systemDesign → archDesign) → URL writes `?funcCat=workTask&subCat=systemDesign&l2=archDesign`; both filters compose if Browse is also selected.
  - `/mcp-panorama` — global aside is gone, page-owned `LayerSidebar` has the full gutter; TopBar nav still reachable for jumping back to /extensions.
  - All three themes (Ivory / Dark / Mono Clean) read correctly — accent restraint preserved in the new function-tree.

## Critical files

- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — primary nav added back
- `app/components/layout/Sidebar.vue` — Explore section removed, function-tree restored, "All Extensions" dropped
